commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c056c960885712d3a1e52cc46b9ace8f6104964d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Windows `schtasks /Query` fallback in `src/infra/supervisor-markers.ts` so env-less gateway processes can be treated as Scheduled Task supervised.

PR surface: Source +35. Total +35 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main returns disabled on win32 without Scheduled Task markers, while the related issue comment identifies the env-less manually started gateway with an installed task. I did not run live Windows proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor Windows proof plus maintainer review of the profile-aware Scheduled Task detection contract before merge; automation cannot supply the external setup proof.

target.title: fix(supervisor): probe schtasks directly when env vars are missing
target.body:
## Summary

On Windows, `detectRespawnSupervisor()` relied solely on environment variables (`OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_NAME`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND`) to detect scheduled task supervision. When the gateway was started manually (e.g. `openclaw.mjs gateway`) instead of through the scheduled task, these env vars are absent, causing the restart logic to fall through to the unsafe detached-respawn path — which itself fails on win32 with `"opts is not defined"`.

This fix adds a synchronous `schtasks /Query` probe as a final fallback on win32 when no service markers are present. The probe detects the `OpenClaw Gateway` scheduled task directly, allowing the restart to use the safe schtasks restart path instead of failing.

## Changes

- **`src/infra/supervisor-markers.ts`**: Added `probeWindowsScheduledTask()` helper that spawns `schtasks.exe /Query /TN <taskName>` with a 3s timeout. Added fallback logic in `detectRespawnSupervisor()` on win32: if env hints and service markers are both absent, probe schtasks directly. Explicitly returns `null` when service markers are present but don't match (e.g. `marker=worker`), to avoid false-positive detection.

## Test plan

- [x] Existing tests pass (no behavioral change for environments with correct env vars)
- [ ] `pnpm test` on the test file to verify
- [ ] Manual verification on Windows: gateway started without env vars → `detectRespawnSupervisor()` returns `"schtasks"` when the scheduled task exists

## Fixes

Fixes #95072

## Note

This replaces the approach in #95075 (which re-introduced unsafe detached respawn). This fix is safe because it keeps the schtasks restart path — the same path used when env vars are correctly set.
